s1_reasoning: Both primary hotspots (CalcFBHourglassForceForElems and CalcKinematicsForElems) are dominated by floating-point operations per element with regular access patterns. Memory access is structured (gather/scatter) but arithmetic intensity is high (~10-20 FLOPs/byte). The inner loops have minimal branching and high operation count.

hotspots.reason: Heaviest computational kernel with nested loops over elements (numElem), hourglass modes (4), and nodes (8). Performs 192 FP operations per element per mode, totaling ~768 FP ops per element. Contains gather/scatter memory access patterns.

Secondary hotspot with gather operations for node coordinates/velocities and significant FP arithmetic for volume/strain calculations. Computes cross products, determinants, and velocity gradients per element.
Triple-nested loops for mesh initialization and connectivity building. Memory-bound due to large array writes but only executed once.

bottleneck_type.reasoning: Both primary hotspots (CalcFBHourglassForceForElems and CalcKinematicsForElems) are dominated by floating-point operations per element with regular access patterns. Memory access is structured (gather/scatter) but arithmetic intensity is high (~10-20 FLOPs/byte). The inner loops have minimal branching and high operation count.

gpu_suitability.reasoning: The computation is element-parallel with regular data access patterns. High arithmetic intensity matches GPU strengths. Gather/scatter operations can be efficiently handled through GPU shared memory.
gpu_suitability.recommended_approach: Port using CUDA/OpenCL: 1) Assign one thread block per element, 2) Use shared memory for node data gathering, 3) Implement atomicAdd for force scattering to global memory, 4) Optimize memory coalescing by reordering element processing.
